-rw-rw-r--+ 35 3646 1040 6.3M Apr 18 23:06 articles/2026/02/01/articles.jsonl
1866 articles/2026/02/01/articles.jsonl

Updates made:
- Champions League draw questions: final league table put Real Madrid 9th, Inter 10th, PSG 11th, Newcastle 12th, Juventus 13th, Atletico 14th, Atalanta 15th, Leverkusen 16th, with Benfica 24th and Bodo/Glimt 23rd. Later active market text explicitly references Benfica vs Real Madrid on Feb. 17, so moved Real's playoff opponent and Benfica's first-leg opponent strongly to each other. Newcastle first leg is now Monaco or Baku from the PSG/Newcastle vs Monaco/Qarabag draw pair. Juventus's tie is against Galatasaray or Club Brugge; because the title says "eliminate Juventus", kept the upset clubs ahead of Juventus despite Juventus being seeded.
- Australian Open men: Sinner beat Shelton in straight sets; Djokovic was very lucky after Musetti retired while two sets up, and Djokovic has a toe blister. Semis are Alcaraz-Zverev and Sinner-Djokovic, with Alcaraz/Sinner close and Djokovic/Zverev as smaller live tails.
- Super Bowl: Drake Maye appeared on the projected injury report as limited with a right-shoulder issue. Raised Seattle slightly again.

Watched but no update:
- Epstein/Commerce Secretary, Crans-Montana skier crash, Pat Cummins replacement, Ukrainian miners-bus city, Balochistan secured town, market-fire city, Grammy Album of the Year, Norway corruption, strategic-minerals stockpile, Libya killing and English League Cup final had no Jan. 28 signal strong enough to move.

## 2026-01-29 Update

Resolution events:
- Epstein/Commerce Secretary resolved to Howard Lutnick, matching the prior leader. Lesson: when the wording says "Trump Commerce Secretary" and the relevant correspondence is contemporary Trump-world, the current/second-term Commerce Secretary can beat the older first-term holder even if the underlying Epstein date is old.
- Crans-Montana skier crash resolved to Lindsey Vonn, matching the lead. Lesson: high-profile comeback skiers deserve meaningful mass in injury/crash markets even when the event signal is thin.
- Champions League draw cluster resolved well after the Jan. 28 table/cross-market update: Real Madrid vs Benfica, Benfica vs Real Madrid, Newcastle first leg in Baku, and Galatasaray as the club that eliminates Juventus. Lesson: active future-dated market titles can contain useful cross-market clues; the Benfica-vs-Real racist-remark question was decisive evidence for the playoff draw.

Updates made:
- Pat Cummins replacement: Jan. 1 squad reporting showed Nathan Ellis was already in Australia's provisional squad, so he is unlikely to be the official replacement. Replaced the stale Ellis tail with outside pace options led by Sean Abbott, then Spencer Johnson, Ben Dwarshuis, Jason Behrendorff and Lance Morris.
- Australian Open men: Jan. 29 previews emphasized Sinner's five-match winning streak over Djokovic and Djokovic's lucky Musetti retirement/physical concerns, while Alcaraz remains setless against Zverev. Moved Alcaraz and Sinner level at 40%, lowered Djokovic.
- Super Bowl: both Maye and Darnold appeared on injury reports, offsetting some of the previous Maye-only move. Eased Seattle from 72% to 70%.

Watched but no update:
- Ukrainian miners-bus city, Balochistan secured town, Grammy Album of the Year, market-fire city, strategic-minerals stockpile, Norway corruption, Libya killing/forensics, English League Cup final, Gaza mission troops and southern Lebanon abduction group had no Jan. 29 signal strong enough to move.

## 2026-01-30 Update

Resolution events:
- Pat Cummins replacement resolved to Ben Dwarshuis. The Jan. 29 correction helped by adding him, but I still overweighted Sean Abbott. Lesson: for Australia squad injury replacements in T20 conditions, left-arm domestic/franchise pace options such as Ben Dwarshuis and Spencer Johnson need more mass, especially when the obvious named seamers are already in the squad.

Updates made:
- Australian Open men: both semifinals went five sets. Alcaraz beat Zverev after a 5h27 match with cramp/medical-timeout concerns; Djokovic beat Sinner in five and reached the final. Moved the market to Alcaraz 60%, Djokovic 40%.
- Grammy Album of the Year: a Jan. 30 Independent preview tipped Bad Bunny's "Debi Tirar Mas Fotos" for Album of the Year, adding to earlier Lady Gaga/Kendrick arguments. Raised Bad Bunny to level with Kendrick and close behind Gaga.
- Super Bowl: Drake Maye missed Friday practice with a right-shoulder issue plus illness, though the Patriots said availability was not a major concern. Raised Seattle to 73%.

Watched but no update:
- Ukrainian miners-bus city, Balochistan secured town, market-fire city, strategic-minerals stockpile, Norway corruption, Libya killing/forensics, English League Cup final, Gaza mission troops, southern Lebanon abduction group and Myanmar panel had no Jan. 30 signal strong enough to move.

## 2026-01-31 Update

Resolution events:
- Ukrainian miners-bus city resolved to Ternivka. I missed by focusing on larger Dnipropetrovsk mining/industrial cities such as Pavlohrad and Marganets. Lesson: for Dnipropetrovsk mining-transport attacks, include smaller coal-mining towns around Pavlohrad such as Ternivka and Pershotravensk.
- Balochistan secured town resolved to Nushki. I missed by sticking to Kalat/Khuzdar. Lesson: Balochistan multi-day battle/security-force questions need Nushki as a material town prior.
- Grammy Album of the Year resolved to Bad Bunny. The Jan. 30 Independent preview was the decisive late signal, but I left Gaga fractionally ahead. Lesson: when multiple previews split, a latest direct "will win AOTY" forecast close to the ceremony should move the named artist to at least co-lead.
- Australian Open men's title resolved to Carlos Alcaraz, matching the post-semifinal leader.

Updates made:
- None. Jan. 31 searches did not produce a strong named signal for active unresolved questions.

Watched but no update:
- Strategic-minerals stockpile name, Norwegian corruption investigation, market-fire city, Libya killing/forensics, English League Cup final, Clippers-Harden trade player, Brazilian bus-crash state, Myanmar panel, Ukrainian market shelling, Supreme Court map case, Super Bowl injuries, Gaza mission troops and southern Lebanon abduction group had no Jan. 31 signal strong enough to move.

input.command:
/usr/bin/bash -lc "python - <<'PY'
import pandas as pd
m=pd.read_csv('market.csv', dtype={'qid':str})
print('rows',len(m),'active',(~m.is_resolved).sum(),'resolved',m.is_resolved.sum(),'no_pred_active',((~m.is_resolved)&(m.num_predictions==0)).sum())
active=m[~m.is_resolved].copy(); active['rd']=pd.to_datetime(active.resolution_date, errors='coerce')
for _,r in active.sort_values('rd').head(75).iterrows():
    print('\\n',r.qid, r.rd.date() if pd.notna(r.rd) else None, 'preds', r.num_predictions)
    print(r.title[:300])
    print('agg', r.market_aggregate)
PY"

blocks.text: Feb. 1 resolved the Norway and strategic-minerals questions; both were misses, with Thorbjorn Jagland and Project Vault. I’m now scanning Feb. 1 reporting for the Feb. 2-3 cluster: League Cup, Libya, market fire, Clippers trade, Brazil crash, Myanmar panel, Supreme Court map, Ukrainian shelling, and related active questions.

input.command:
/usr/bin/bash -lc "python - <<'PY'
import json,textwrap
from pathlib import Path
patterns=[
 ('league_cup_final', ['League Cup final','Manchester City']),
 ('league_cup_arsenal', ['Manchester City','Arsenal','League Cup']),
 ('league_cup_chels', ['Manchester City','Chelsea','League Cup']),
 ('clippers_harden', ['Clippers','Harden','trade']),
 ('tyler_herro', ['Tyler Herro','Clippers']),
 ('andrew_wiggins', ['Andrew Wiggins','Clippers']),
 ('libya_haddad', ['Libya','Haddad','killed']),
 ('libya_killed', ['Al Jazeera Arabic','Libya','killed']),
 ('libya_forensic', ['Libya','forensic experts']),
 ('zintan_forensic', ['Zintan','forensic']),
 ('market_fire', ['huge fire','market']),
 ('dhaka_fire', ['Dhaka','market','fire']),
 ('lagos_fire', ['Lagos','market','fire']),
 ('brazil_bus_crash', ['Brazil','bus crash','16']),
 ('minas_bus', ['Minas Gerais','bus crash']),
 ('bahia_bus', ['Bahia','bus crash']),
 ('myanmar_panel', ['Myanmar','five-member panel']),
 ('security_peace_commission', ['Security and Peace Commission','Myanmar']),
 ('supreme_map', ['Supreme Court','congressional map']),
 ('louisiana_map', ['Louisiana','congressional map']),
 ('north_carolina_map', ['North Carolina','congressional map']),
 ('ukraine_market_shelling', ['Ukraine','market shelling']),
 ('kostiantynivka_market', ['Kostiantynivka','market']),
 ('kharkiv_market', ['Kharkiv','market shelling']),
 ('saleh_execution', ['Saleh Mohammadi','public execution']),
 ('lyon_deranque', ['Quentin Deranque','Lyon']),
 ('saif_buried', ['Saif al-Islam','buried']),
 ('super_bowl', ['Super Bowl','Seahawks','Patriots']),
 ('narges_exile', ['Narges Mohammadi','exiled']),
 ('washington_post_publisher', ['Washington Post','Will Lewis','publisher']),
 ('russian_officer_moscow', ['Russian','officer','shot','Moscow']),
]
res=[]
with Path('articles/2026/02/01/articles.jsonl').open() as f:
  for i,line in enumerate(f,1):
    try:a=json.loads(line)
    except: continue
    text=(a.get('title') or '')+'\\n'+(a.get('content') or '')
    low=text.lower()
    for label,terms in patterns:
      if all(t.lower() in low for t in terms):
        pos=min([low.find(t.lower()) for t in terms if low.find(t.lower())>=0] or [0])
        snip=text[max(0,pos-300):pos+1300].replace('\\n',' ')
        res.append((label,i,a.get('date_publish'),a.get('source'),a.get('title'),snip))
        break
from collections import defaultdict
counts=defaultdict(int)
print('matches',len(res))
for label,i,date,src,title,snip in res:
  if counts[label]>=8: continue
  counts[label]+=1
  print(f\"\\n## {label} line {i} pub={date} src={src}\\nTITLE: {title}\\nSNIP: {textwrap.shorten(snip, width=1500, placeholder=' ...')}\")
print('\\ncounts')
for k in sorted(counts): print(k,counts[k])
PY"

TITLE: Super Bowl LX coach reportedly to take Raiders job after game
SNIP: Super Bowl LX coach reportedly to take Raiders job after game NEWYou can now listen to Fox News articles! The Las Vegas Raiders were one of the final teams in need of a head coach and on Sunday the organization reportedly found their guy. The Raiders appeared to be zeroing in on Seattle Seahawks offensive coordinator Klint Kubiak, according to multiple reports on Sunday. Kubiak won’t be able to officially accept the job until Super Bowl LX is played. The Seahawks are going up against the New England Patriots. CLICK HERE FOR MORE SPORTS COVERAGE ON FOXNEWS.COM Kubiak intends to work out a deal with the Raiders after a second interview over the weekend, ESPN reported. The Seahawks were third in points scored and eighth in yards gained during the 2025 season. Seattle won the NFC West and defeated the Los Angeles Rams in the NFC Championship to earn their first Super Bowl appearance since the 2014 season. DRAKE MAYE 'SUPER BOWL' GUY, FORMER COACH SAYS AS PATRIOTS QB REACHES NFL'S BIGGEST STAGE Kubiak is in his first year as Seattle’s offensive coordinator. He served in the same role for the New Orleans Saints in 2024 and was the Minnesota Vikings’ offensive coordinator in 2021. Should the deal with the Raiders be finalized, Las Vegas will be his sixth team since that season. He was w

## super_bowl line 595 pub=2026-01-31 src=www.foxnews.com
TITLE: Seahawks cornerback Tariq Woolen fined for taunting Rams bench
SNIP: Seahawks cornerback Tariq Woolen fined for taunting Rams bench NEWYou can now listen to Fox News articles! Seattle Seahawks defensive back Tariq Woolen was fined $17,398 Saturday for a taunting penalty during the NFC championship game against the Los Angeles Rams. Woolen's penalty came at a critical point with less than three minutes in the third quarter. After the Seahawks stopped the Rams on third down with an 11-point lead, it appeared Seattle would get an opportunity to put the game out of reach. But Woolen jawed at the Rams bench while backpedaling with his hands behind his back after the play. CLICK HERE FOR MORE SPORTS COVERAGE ON FOXNEWS.COM So, instead of forcing a punt back to the Seattle offense, the penalty gave the Rams an automatic first down. LA scored just a play later to cut the lead to one possession. Woolen yelled at his teammates on the sideline after the Rams touchdown and was the focus of immense criticism on social media. After the game, Woolen took to social media to address the situation and the backlash he faced, appearing to offer an apology in meme form, while also praising the connection with his teammates amid the sideline altercation. ## super_bowl line 606 pub=2026-02-01 src=www.foxnews.com
TITLE: Super Bowl champion on which tight ends lead talented position group
SNIP: Super Bowl champion on which tight ends lead talented position group There is no shortage of talent at the tight end position in the NFL. Kansas City Chiefs standout Travis Kelce and San Francisco 49ers star George Kittle are now considered the elder statesman at the position. Each is expected to end up in Canton, with Hall of Fame gold jackets waiting for them not long after they decide to step away from the game. Tyler Warren made an immediate impact in his rookie season with the Indianapolis Colts. The position also features young players who had highly productive first seasons, including Atlanta Falcons tight end Kyle Pitts, Arizona tight end Trey McBride and emerging star Brock Bowers. CLICK HERE FOR MORE SPORTS COVERAGE ON FOXNEWS.COM Brent Jones knows what it takes to succeed at tight end. A three-time Super Bowl champion during his 11-year run with the 49ers, Jones said he’s impressed by the talent at the position in today’s NFL. Jones spoke to Fox News Digital on behalf of Athletes in Action and the Super Bowl Breakfast. He has been tapped to emcee the 38th edition of the annual event, which is scheduled for next week in Santa Clara, California. Several tight ends put together productive seasons in 2025, with five players at the position racking up at least 800 receiving

TITLE: Ex-UNC coach reveals prediction about Drake Maye’s success
SNIP: Ex-UNC coach reveals prediction about Drake Maye’s success NEWYou can now listen to Fox News articles! Mack Brown knows first-hand what type of player Drake Maye can be. Before the New England Patriots selected Maye in the first round of the 2024 NFL Draft, Brown coached Maye at North Carolina. Next week, Maye will play in the first Super Bowl of his career. While Maye reached the game’s biggest stage in just his second NFL season, Brown was always confident his former quarterback would have success at the next level. "I told scouts when they would come through that he's a Super Bowl guy, He'll win a Super Bowl," Brown told WRAL. "I didn't know he was going to have a chance to be the second-youngest guy to ever do it." CLICK HERE FOR MORE SPORTS COVERAGE ON FOXNEWS.COM Maye finished his two-year run as the Tar Heels’ starting quarterback with 62 touchdowns. He threw for 8,018 yards over his three years in a North Carolina uniform. PATRIOTS' DRAKE MAYE MISSES PRACTICE WITH INJURY, ILLNESS JUST 9 DAYS BEFORE SUPER BOWL Before returning to UNC, Brown spent 16 years at Texas, where he coached several highly touted NFL-bound quarterbacks, including Vince Young and Colt McCoy. Brown believes Maye is on a path that could eventually place him among the game’s all-time greats. ## league_cup_arsenal line 1018 pub=2026-02-01 src=www.independent.co.uk
TITLE: Why Arsenal need to follow Pep Guardiola’s example to end Premier League drought
SNIP: Why Arsenal need to follow Pep Guardiola’s example to end Premier League drought Arsenal’s win over Leeds was not so much about steadying the ship. That is because it more felt like in recent weeks the door was ready to blow on an engine room full up with steam rather than the boat rocking such was the pressure building. Their victory at Elland Road, a four-goal rout of the Premier League strugglers, put them back on course in the title race and eased fears they were starting to sink under the weight of apprehension which was building around North London after a three-game winless run gave Manchester City and Aston Villa more than a bit of hope. Now as they prepare for a Carabao Cup semi-final second leg against Chelsea, Mikel Arteta’s men have the opportunity to return to Wembley for a major final for the first time since 2020 and, with that, the chance to get their hands on silverware. Going all the way would end a six-year wait for a trophy – do not listen to managers who count the Community Shield – and, while it may not totally remove the anxiety at a club that crave the Premier League title more, it would give this group of players the taste of winning and drop the tag of serial runners-up. That, in turn, could give them a hunger for even more and be a catalyst for getting over

## super_bowl line 1022 pub=2026-02-01 src=www.independent.co.uk
TITLE: Super Bowl week sightseeing and adventure goes well beyond football in the San Francisco Bay Area
SNIP: Super Bowl week sightseeing and adventure goes well beyond football in the San Francisco Bay Area Take a stroll down The Embarcadero on San Francisco's spectacular waterfront to the Ferry Plaza Farmers Market for plentiful food options, people watching and souvenir shopping. Travel farther outside the city for renowned wine tasting or a breathtaking drive along the coastline. Fans coming to the Bay Area for the Super Bowl will have no shortage of options for great eats and outdoor adventure, all within a couple of hours of San Francisco. Sure, the week centers around football — it's Patriots vs. Seahawks at Levi's Stadium in Santa Clara on Feb. 8 — yet anyone visiting this week can seek out entertainment ahead of Super Bowl Sunday. Make sure to hit the Ferry Building on Tuesday, Thursday or Saturday for one of the most famous farmers markets in the country, with both indoor and outdoor food and shopping options. From world-renowned vineyards and wineries to the north in the Napa Valley region to spectacular ocean vistas to the south along 17-Mile Drive on the Monterey Peninsula providing views of picturesque Pebble Beach Golf Links, there's something for everybody. There is a fee for cars on 17-Mile Drive, while walking and biking are free. It can be foggy during the winter seaso

## super_bowl line 1142 pub=2026-01-30 src=www.cbsnews.com
TITLE: Watch Live: New England Patriots Super Bowl sendoff rally at Gillette Stadium
SNIP: Watch Live: New England Patriots Super Bowl sendoff rally at Gillette Stadium The New England Patriots are leaving for the Super Bowl Sunday morning and there's a sendoff rally at Gillette Stadium. If you're not able to be in Foxboro for it, you can watch the rally live on CBS News Boston in the embedded video when the program begins at 10 a.m. The parking lots at Gillette Stadium opened for fans at 8 a.m. and the stadium gates opened at 9 a.m. The entertainment begins at 9:30 a.m. with the Patriots cheerleaders and some former players making appearances. Then at 10 a.m., the Dropkick Murphys will entertain the crowd for about half an hour. Patriots owner Robert Kraft and head coach Mike Vrabel will address the crowd at 10:30 a.m. Patriots captains Drake Maye, Marcus Jones, Hunter Henry, Robert Spillane, Harold Landry III and Brenden Schooler are also expected to speak, according to the team. The program ends at 11:15 a.m. and the Patriots will start boarding their buses. They're scheduled to leave for T.F. Green Airport in Warwick, Rhode Island around 11:30 a.m. The Patriots will face the Seattle Seahawks in Super Bowl LX on Sunday, February 8 at 6:30 pm. at Levi's Stadium in Santa Clara, California.

counts
league_cup_arsenal 1
super_bowl 8
